raw_input:
[5.0/5] Working Well We picked this up to use on dogs and cats. Most of them are short-haired, but one of the cats is a bit fluffy. They're all happy with the brush (one cat and one dog get freaked out with certain brushes and I'm not sure why).

The self-cleaning feature is pretty cool. You're not stuck plucking out the pet hair because it pushes it out for you. Have not had any issue with that either.

However, the bristles are quite bendable. One of my kiddos pushed on some and bent them out of place. It looks bad, but it didn't impact the usability of it and the self-cleaning feature still works. Not really an issue, but something to be mindful of. [5.0/5] Nice! I like this brush for my small, short-hair dog. It helps remove his loose hair and he seems to enjoy it when I brush him. I like that I can push a button and it pushes the hair to the ends of the bristles so that I can easily remove the hair. It doesn't get all of the hair, but it is a lot more effective than a normal brush. Overall, I'm really happy with this brush and would get one like it again. [4.0/5] Ejection system works well but times are actually too long for long haired cats. So I have a cat which is probably classified as a Norwegian forest cat breed with very long hair. And I really need to comb him to ensure I can get whatever hair he sheds to control hairballs. This brush does do a fairly good job but with my cat specifically the problem is that the tines are all bent in one direction. Because of this you have to draw the brush downward on the cat in a smooth motion where the handle is always oriented downward in a line with your stroke. By that I mean you can't hold the brush sideways or you can't pull the brush upside down and just use it any direction you want you must always draw the brush downward in direction consistent with the handle, because else the bent tips work against you.

On top of that the tines are actually a little overly long. I found that if I partially engage the ejection button but I can much more easily brush my cat because it artificially shortens the tines. When the tines are fully engaged I basically catch too much and the they end up trying to bend versus navigate their way through the fur.

It's possible that I'll be forced to switch to a normal bristle brush and try that but I have had high hopes for this because I'm used to using stiff brushes in order to remove excess fur from pets before.

So I guess if I have one complaint it would be basically that I wish the times weren't bent and we're about 30% shorter. Then I could move the brush and any orientation I want and for my pet it would work very well. The deeper tines tend to get just caught and don't drag through the fur as easily. [3.0/5] Great fur release button This tiny, round brush is in festive colors. The bumblebee pattern is indeed adorable. I especially love the one button fur release design. Simply push down to get rid of the fur pad is definitely convenient. However it's a little small to use when I tried to brush my pyrenes puppy. It works nice for smaller area that maybe I want to strengthen in brushing. But as for whole body I wish it's somewhat bigger to cover more range. [5.0/5] I love this [[VIDEOID:81a04eff843f7893740591f0fd77ef12]] This is the best pet brush I have used. Easy to hold and the bumble bee detail is an added touch of cuteness. The fact that one push and all the hair comes out at once, is amazing. So easy and simple. [5.0/5] Works smoothly, Bonus it's cute! One of my cats is chunky so she doesn't give herself a bath that often so this brush works so well! It's well made and the best part is the push button to push the hair out of the brush. I hated having to pull the hair out of the brush every time. I think it also brushes well and picks up most of the hair on my shorthaired cats. It's not a huge area with the brush part so I think this one is best for cats and small dogs. [5.0/5] No surprises! This product looks, feels, and works as indicated in the description; no surprises, and very easy to use and clean. [5.0/5] Super cute AND functional! With 4 kitties who share our home- two of which are super fluffy and all four are super 'sheddy' - I'm always on the hunt for ways to manage the fur tubleweeds that roll through our home! I ordered this brush because....well....I had nothing to lose! And boy - I was happily surprised!

Pros:
* The brushing area is nice size - it allows me to brush a large area at one time, which is key when dealing with kitties who don't love to be brushed.

* The bristles have tiny rubber tips, which is a small detail that makes a huge difference! This little 'bumper' seems to be much more appealing to our older kitties - perhaps their skin is more sensitive or fragile. Also, for our younger more rambunctious crew, it's not unusual to find minor 'battle wounds' (don't worry, nothing serious and always in good fun). Having this little rubber tip protector prevents from tearing off any scabs as they heal.

* It works! The 'self cleaning' aspect of this brush DOES work! With a wide variety of fur lengths and textures in our 'pack', I can attest to the fact that it catches it all, making it so much easier to keep clean. No more digging into the spikey needley prongs, ruining your fingers as you try to fish out all the pieces of fur! Hooray!

Cons:
* This isn't TRULY a con, but rather a small detail that would have made this tool even better....a cushioned brush. Several brushes that we've liked have 'cushioned' brush heads (where the bristles attach). This allows the brush to contour better to the kitty's body. That's the only thing I can think of that would have made this brush better.

Overall, this cute brush earns 15 paws of approval from our Pack! (One is a tripawd....)

If you find this review helpful, please let us know! [5.0/5] Efficient I love this and so do my cats

Pros- Easy to clean

Cons- A little big but not by too much [4.0/5] The cute isn't a cover up... A good number of times when you see something as cute as this little bee-esque slicker brush, the manufacturer is covering up a shoddy product and hoping the "awwww, it's SO CUUUUUUTE!!!!!" factor will overwhelm the buyer so much they don't notice and/or care when the product gives up the ghost after only a few uses. So yes, choosing this slicker based on looks probably wasn't the smartest of choices, but in the end it seems to be a good one. This slicker brush is rather nice (and should be for the $20 price tag). It's comfortable to hold. There is weight to it so this doesn't feel flimsy to where the grooming head will break off if you try to comb out a snarl in your pet's fur, or like the body is the thinnest molded plastic they could get away with so it didn't break just removing it from the packaging. Another plus is the end of the tines are tipped/dipped in protective little drops of plastic so the sharp metal ends don't harm your pet. I've always avoided those slickers as they seem like some kind of torture device - how can bare metal ends be comfortable when raked over skin?

For taking care of loose hair, this did a bang up job on our two Maine Coons, including the one who absolutely loathes - and I mean to the depths of H.E. double hockey sticks with a side of utter disgust - being brushed. She was adopted from the shelter as an abused kitty from the home of an older cat hoarder, and whatever happened during her first six years of life there were traumatic enough that she will do her best to eviscerate anyone who tries to groom her long fur beyond the area of her neck. She actually lets us very gently use this on the rest of her back and legs and tail for around 45-60 seconds before flipping out, and that's a big improvement! Who knows, maybe she's as amused by the itty bitty bee antenna as we are, but this is the first slicker brush (even with the safety tipped teeth) she's let us use on her back, sides, and legs. The other Maine Coon (a big chonker of a guy) lays on his back with his legs stretched out and purrs like a race car when he gets groomed, so it's not a surprise that he loves this slicker as well. Cleaning the hair out is easy. Just press the button, the plastic disk pushes up, and the hair is forced off of the tines for easy removal. A few of the finer hairs may stick to the plastic or around the tines, but nothing major. When the slicker gets dirty between the base and the underside of the plastic disk, one of those super skinny reusable straw scrubby brushes and a bit of dish soap does the trick. We clip a wooden clothespin on either side of the plastic disk to keep it raised so the tines can air dry and not rust from trapped moisture. Since the tines all seem to be securely anchored to the base of the slicker head, we aren't too concerned about one being pulled out while trying to work through a tangle. It's still a good idea to check the tines every so often to make sure they are all there, none are loose, and that none of the little safety tips at the end of the tines have come off.

The only thing that could improve this slicker is a price reduction. A pair of googly eyes wouldn't hurt, either. [4.0/5] Easy to Clean The brush itself works fairly well on my short-haired kitties. It's not nearly as good as my Furminator, but my cats freak out with that brush. This gets the loose hair off, but it doesn't pick up everything. I actually brushed my cats against the fur growth. This released more fur. I do have to say, cleaning it is so easy. You just push the little button, and the plate pushes up past the bristles with the fur clinging to it. I think this would work great on thicker-haired pets. It's not terrible on short-haired animals, but it's not a miracle brush either. Overall, it's a pretty good brush with some limitations. [5.0/5] Great brush Does what you want gets hair and easy to remove and clean [5.0/5] A handy and fun brush for my long-haired dog Honestly, it doesn’t really matter to me what this brush looks like, but it is very cute. :)

I am a sheltie person, and they get a thick undercoat and shed a lot, mostly seasonally. Did my dog love the brush? Well, she’s pretty particular and doesn’t really like to get brushed so much. But she did cooperate and it wasn’t annoying to her. As the bristle ends are covered in little plastic tips, this is not sharp if it makes contact with the dog or cat’s skin.

For me, I love it, It’s very easy to use, fits great in my hand, and the button to clean the fur off it is AMAZING. Just press the button and the fur basically ejects. It also has a good-sized brush head, so it provides wide brushing coverage, and it is very through and goes through tangles well. (Though you should cut any intense tangles first.)

You may notice that my review says: Vine Customer Review of Free Product
I try not to let that influence my reviews, because I’ve bought a lot of products over the years and I’m very price conscious. I do wonder about the price point of this compared to other similar brushes. This one looks great, feels great, and works great so maybe it is better than those that cost less (I haven’t tested those). But—it’s always best to compare prices and read reviews.

For me, I love this; it makes brushing easy, and will replace all my previous dog brushes. [5.0/5] cute and works great! really gentle brush that works great! [5.0/5] Super Easy To Clean! This is the perfect brush to add to your pet grooming arsenal.

I use this brush as an indoor brush, I have another that cuts and lifts loose hair for outdoors. This brush is nice because of the trigger that easily releases all the pet hair. It is so convenient! It's a real cure design too. I was initially worried that the antennae on top would easily break off but the are made of a flexible rubber. The bristles are made of a durable metal with a white coating on the tips so they're not so sharp.

Overall, this is a great first pet brush, or a great addition to the supplies you already have. You can never have too many brushes. I recommend this to all the pet lovers out there. [5.0/5] How cute is this brush I ordered 3 similar brushes of the 3 this one is definitely the cutest. It's also a higher quality that the other 3. It works well and is easy to clean. This would make a great gift for a new pet parent! [5.0/5] Love it! My cats love it! It’s easy to clean and it catches all the hair!!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers are very happy with this pet brush, which features a bumblebee design and a self-cleaning button that pushes hair off the bristles. Most reviewers highlight the easy cleaning feature as a standout benefit, noting that a simple button press removes collected fur without having to pick it out by hand. The brush is generally well-liked for use on cats and small to medium dogs, and many customers appreciate that the bristle tips are coated in small rubber or plastic caps, making it gentler on pets' skin.

Several reviewers note that the brush works best on short to medium-haired pets. Those with long-haired cats or dogs had mixed results, with one reviewer noting the bristles are too long and tend to catch and bend rather than glide through thick fur. A couple of customers also mentioned the brush head is on the smaller side, which can make full-body grooming of larger pets time-consuming.

A few minor complaints came up across reviews. The bristles can bend if pressed too hard, and some felt the price was a bit high. One reviewer wished the brush head were cushioned to better contour to a pet's body.

Despite these small drawbacks, the vast majority of reviewers would recommend this brush and praise it for being cute, functional, comfortable to hold, and easy to clean.
